summary: Recent updates to Firefox's WebRender, the GPU compositor that handles how graphics are displayed on your screen, focused on improving support for different YUV formats, which are important for video processing. Specifically, the addition of **NV16 YUV format support** and fixes to the **P210 format** enhance the browser's ability to efficiently display video content, ensuring better quality and compatibility with modern web media. Changes also included refining how shapes are rendered, such as using rounded rectangles and clip masks, which enhances the visual fidelity of web graphics. These improvements contribute to smoother video playback and more visually appealing web elements, ultimately enriching the user experience.
